relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The great city-system seated on waters is shown for judgment.
Kings and earth-dwellers are intoxicated by her power.
In wilderness John sees the woman on scarlet beast.
Luxury and impurity fill her golden cup.
Her mystery-name is Babylon the Great.
She is drunk on the life of holy witnesses.
The beast fascinates earth-dwellers and moves toward ruin.
Wisdom sees mountains and kings in the symbol.
The beast belongs to the cycle and goes to ruin.
They give power to the beast.
They war with the Lamb, and the Lamb conquers with the called and trustworthy.
Waters are peoples, crowds, nations, and tongues.
Empire turns on its own seductive city.
Even this serves completion of Divine words.
The woman is the great city reigning over kings.

contemplative_note: Revelation 17 unveils Babylon as seductive empire: luxury, intoxication, witness-blood, and self-devouring power under the Lamb’s greater reign.
